assertions.text: V1 was traveling eastbound on the roadway.
The roadway was a two-lane, non-divided, bidirectional roadway.
The roadway had a slight curve to the left along V1's path.
The roadway had a positive grade (uphill in the direction of travel).
V1 departed the roadway to the right (off the eastbound lane).
V1's frontal plane impacted a large tree (front-of-vehicle impact with a tree).
After departing the roadway to the right, V1's front impacted the tree.
V1 came to final rest in the eastbound lane of travel.
At final rest V1 was facing south.
There was a single impact recorded (V1 impacting a large tree).

verdicts.evidence: The telemetry provides only scalar vehicle speed ([98.0 km/h and 92.0 km/h](T2)) and no absolute heading or GPS track in the available channels, so direction-of-travel (eastbound) cannot be determined from the provided data.
Roadway geometry and lane configuration are not present in the telemetry channels; available signals ([speed and crash pulse summary](T1, T2)) do not encode road classification.
No yaw, steering-angle, or GPS track channels were available to show curvature; only crash-pulse and speed are recorded ([delta-V channels and speed samples](T1, T2)).
No inclinometer, altitude, or GPS elevation trend provided; available channels ([speed, delta-V, brake](T1, T2)) cannot determine road grade.

Lateral/off-road departure side requires position or heading track; only a small lateral delta-V was recorded ([delta_v_lat final = -7.0 km/h](T2)) but that does not specify which side of the road the vehicle left.

The crash pulse shows a large negative longitudinal delta-V consistent with a frontal impact ([max_delta_v_long_kmh = -95.0 km/h at t=0.12 s, final -92.0 km/h](T1, T2)), and the EDR notes one event ([num_events_recorded = 1](T1)), but sensor data cannot identify the struck object as a tree or confirm the exact struck plane without additional mounting-frame orientation or scene data.

Sequence of departure then front impact requires positional track; sensors show a crash pulse consistent with a frontal impact ([delta_v_long peak/final](T1, T2)) but do not record an off-road departure beforehand or the side (right) prior to impact.
Final rest position relative to the roadway is not available in the telemetry; only speed and crash-pulse end-state are in the data ([speed samples and delta-V final](T2)).
Final heading is not recorded in the available channels; no yaw/compass/GPS data are present to support a south-facing final orientation.

The EDR summary reports [num_events_recorded = 1](T1), and the crash-pulse channels show a single dominant longitudinal delta-V peak ([max_abs_value 95.0 km/h, value -95.0 km/h at t=0.12 s and final -92.0 km/h](T2)).

result.rationale: The telemetry clearly records a single, large frontal-oriented crash pulse ([max_delta_v_long_kmh = -95.0 km/h at t=0.12 s and final -92.0 km/h](T1, T2)) and no pre-crash braking ([brake fraction_on=0.0](T2)), supporting that a single severe impact occurred. However, nearly all statements about roadway geometry, direction (eastbound), departure side (right), struck object being a tree, and final resting position/orientation are not verifiable from the available vehicle-frame channels and EDR summary alone; external scene or GPS/yaw data would be needed.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse
